contact.how_to_apply: THE NOVARTIS PATIENT ASSISTANCE FOUNDATION, INC (NPAF) IS COMMITTED TO PROVIDING ACCESS TO NOVARTIS MEDICATIONS FOR THOSE MOST IN NEED. IF YOU ARE EXPERIENCING FINANCIAL HARDSHIP AND HAVE LIMITED OR NO PRESCRIPTION COVERAGE, THEN YOU MAY BE ELIGIBLE TO RECEIVE NOVARTIS MEDICATIONS FOR FREE. TO ENROLL, PLEASE GO TO WWW.PAP.NOVARTIS.COM SELECT YOUR MEDICATION FROM THE LIST AND FOLLOW THE DIRECTIONS PROVIDED.

contact.restrictions: KEY ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA FOR RECEIVING AWARDS: - ALIGN WITH THE FOUNDATION'S VALUES - ALIGN WITH THE FOUNDATION'S MISSION AND THE FOUNDATION'S FOCUS AREA - PROGRAM MUST BE DELIVERED IN THE AREA SURROUNDING CATERPILLAR FACILITY (SOME EXCEPTIONS) - MUST HAVE 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT STATUS OR INTERNATIONAL EQUIVALENT - MUST PROVIDE THE FOLLOWING FINANCIAL DOCUMENTS: (1) EXTERNALLY AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND (2) IRS FULL FORM 990 PREPARED ON THE ACCRUAL METHOD FOR US BASED ORGANIZATIONS - MUST BE ORGANIZATION WITH HEALTHY FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE AND REASONABLE ADMINISTRATIVE OVERHEAD - PROGRAM MUST BE ABLE TO QUANTITATIVELY MEASURE INDICATORS IN FOCUS AREA GENERALLY, AREAS OF NON-ALIGNMENT INCLUDE: - CAPITAL CAMPAIGNS (INFRASTRUCTURE, EXPANSION, RENOVATION, EQUIPMENT, ETC.) - RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATIONS (ORGANIZATIONS CLASSIFIED AS CHURCHES) - SCHOLARSHIPS - DIRECT BENEFIT TO CATERPILLAR, ITS SUBSIDIARIES, DEALERS, EMPLOYEE OR CUSTOMERS - ORGANIZATIONS WITH DISCRIMINATORY POLICIES - HOSPITALS

contact.restrictions: The Anschutz Foundation operates as a general purpose foundation and provides grants to causes and organizations primarily in Colorado that support youth development and education, health and wellness, human services and improving quality of life-with special focus on promoting personal values and responsibilities. The Foundation does not make donations directly to individuals or to any organization that does not qualify as a 501(c)(3) organization under the Internal Revenue Service.
contact.how_to_apply: Realizing that the cost of preparing a grant proposal can be a major expense, the Foundation recommends that new applicants send a one to two page letter of inquiry. The Foundation will use this letter as part of its screening process. Upon review of your letter, a member of the Foundation staff may contact you for further information and provide access to our on-line application which will ask you to provide additional information. Your request will remain under consideration until you receive notification that it has been either denied or awarded.

contact.restrictions: The Amgen Foundation will not consider requests for:- Support to individuals- Fundraising or sports-related events- Corporate sponsorship requests- Religious organizations unless the gift is designated to a program that is secular in nature and benefits a broad range of the community (e.g. soup kitchens and homeless shelters). Additionally, the program must have a formal mission and separate budget and staff.- Political organizations or lobbying activity- Labor unions- Private foundations- Organizations not described in Sections 501(c)(3) and 509(a)(1), (2), (3) of the Internal Revenue Code- Organizations that discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran or disability statuses in the delivery of services and in their employment practices.
contact.how_to_apply: Letters of Intent and Grant Applications should be Submitted Online at http://wwwext.amgen.com/responsibility/health-society/community-investment/amgenfoundation/amgen-foundation-grants.Nearly all Amgen Foundation grants are made by invitation.Within the United States and Puerto Rico, the Foundation will, however, respectfully contemplate a small number of unsolicited requests for local programs whose work is fully aligned to our strategy to strengthen Science Education in the communities where Amgen has a presence. Unsolicited requests are only reviewed on a semiannual basis, in March and September.Organizations that choose to submit an "Unsolicited Letter of Inquiry" must first complete an eligibility quiz before submitting their request. contact.restrictions: THE FOUNDATION PROVIDES FUNDING THROUGH GRANTS FOR RESEARCH AND CESSATION EDUCATION PROJECTS IN ORDER TO ACCELERATE THE GLOBAL DECLINE IN SMOKING. THE FOUNDATION RECOGNIZES THAT SOME GROUPS OF SMOKERS FACE ADDITIONAL CHALLENGES IN BEING ABLE TO SUCCESSFULLY STOP THE USE OF COMBUSTIBLE AND TRADITIONAL TOBACCO PRODUCTS, AND MAY REQUIRE ADDITIONAL SUPPORT AND APPROACHES TO QUIT. The FOUNDATION ALSO RECOGNIZES THAT COUNTRIES AT DIFFERENT LEVELS OF ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT WILL NEED DIFFERENT SOLUTIONS TO EFFECTIVELY REDUCE SMOKING. THE FOUNDATION PROVIDES GRANTS TO SUPPORT WORK THAT IS BEING CONDUCTED BY INDEPENDENT RESEARCH ORGANIZATIONS, ACADEMIC INSTITUTIONS AND CENTERS, AND ORGANIZATIONS IN THE PRIVATE SECTOR. SEE SPECIFIC INFORMATION ABOUT WAYS TO SUBMIT AN APPLICATION AND HOW THE FOUNDATION FUNDS GRANTS ON THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE: HTTPS://WWW.GLOBALACTIONTOENDSMOKING.ORG/GRANTS/
contact.how_to_apply: SEE SPECIFIC GRANTS APPLICATION INFORMATION POSTED ON THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE

contact.restrictions: BOHEMIAN FUND APPLICANTS MUST BE A 501(C)(3) ORGANIZATION OR GOVERNMENTAL UNIT (AS DEFINED IN SECTION 170(C)(1) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE). SOME EXCLUSIONS APPLY. GOVERNMENTAL ENTITIES THAT DO NOT FURTHER A CHARITABLE PURPOSE, FISCALLY SPONSORED PROGRAMS, FAITH-BASED ORGANIZATIONS REQUIRING FAITH-BASED PROGRAMMING PARTICIPATION, PRIVATE SCHOOLS, PRIVATE FOUNDATIONS, AND TYPE III NON-FUNCTIONALLY-INTEGRATED SUPPORTING ORGANIZATIONS ARE INELIGIBLE TO APPLY TO BOHEMIAN FUND. PLEASE SEE OUR FULL GUIDELINES FOR ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS FOR GOVERNMENT ENTITIES. APPLICANTS DON'T NEED TO BE HEADQUARTERED IN FORT COLLINS TO RECEIVE FUNDING, HOWEVER, APPLICANTS MUST SERVE CLIENTS WITHIN THE POUDRE SCHOOL DISTRICT BOUNDARY OR HAVE A DIRECT IMPACT ON INDIVIDUALS IN OUR GRANTMAKING AREA. ORGANIZATIONS MAY ONLY SUBMIT ONE APPLICATION PER GRANT ROUND. (FOR GOVERNMENTAL UNITS, POUDRE SCHOOL DISTRICT, AND COLORADO STATE UNIVERSITY APPLICANTS, ONE APPLICANT PER SCHOOL OR DEPARTMENT IS ALLOWABLE). COM
contact.how_to_apply: BOHEMIAN FUND GRANT APPLICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED THROUGH BOHEMIAN FOUNDATION'S ONLINE GRANTEE PORTAL. ACCESS TO THE PORTAL IS AVAILABLE ON OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.BOHEMIANFOUNDATION.ORG. REQUIRED ATTACHMENTS MAY INCLUDE A DETAILED BUDGET FOR THE PROGRAM/PROJECT, A PRIOR YEAR BUDGET VS ACTUAL FOR THE PROGRAM/PROJECT AND A BOARD OF DIRECTORS LIST. COMMUNITY EVENT FUND GRANT APPLICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED THROUGH BOHEMIAN FOUNDATION'S ONLINE GRANTEE PORTAL. ACCESS TO THE PORTAL IS AVAILABLE ON OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.BOHEMIANFOUNDATION.ORG. REQUIRED ATTACHMENTS INCLUDE A DETAILED EVENT BUDGET AND A LISTING OF ALL EVENT SPONSORSHIP BENEFITS. MUSIC EVENT FUND GRANT APPLICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED THROUGH BOHEMIAN FOUNDATION'S ONLINE GRANTEE PORTAL. ACCESS TO THE PORTAL IS AVAILABLE ON OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.BOHEMIANFOUNDATION.ORG. REQUIRED ATTACHMENTS INCLUDE A DETAILED EVENT BUDGET, A LISTING OF ALL EVENT SPONSORSHIP BENEFITS, AND A PUBLICITY MEMO.

contact.restrictions: THE PG&E CORPORATION FOUNDATION ONLY MAKES GRANTS TO ORGANIZATIONS THAT HOLD CURRENT TAX-EXEMPT STATUS UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OR TO UNITS OF GOVERNMENT (INCLUDING PUBLIC SCHOOLS). A VERY HIGH PERCENTAGE OF THE PG&E CORPORATION FOUNDATION'S GRANTS ARE MADE TO ORGANIZATIONS SOLICITED BY THE PG&E CORPORATION FOUNDATION. THE PG&E CORPORATION FOUNDATION DOES NOT MAKE CONTRIBUTIONS TO OR FOR: - SPECIFIC INDIVIDUALS - TICKETS OR CONTESTS, RAFFLES OR OTHER ACTIVITIES WITH PRIZES - RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATIONS (UNLESS THE REQUEST IS SPECIFICALLY FOR A PROGRAM OFFERED TO THE PUBLIC ON A NONDISCRIMINATORY BASIS AND WITHOUT REGARD TO THE RECIPIENT'S RELIGIOUS AFFILIATION) - ENDOWMENTS - FILMMAKING - DEBT-REDUCTION CAMPAIGNS - POLITICAL OR PARTISAN ORGANIZATIONS OR EVENTS

contact.restrictions: THE ROTARY CLUB OF PETALUMA FOUNDATION AWARDS GRANTS ONLY TO ORGANIZATIONS WITHIN THE PETALUMA SHCOOL DISTRICT ATTENDANCE AREA. APPLICATIONS FROM NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS IN THIS AREA, OTHER THAN SCHOOLS, ARE ALSO ELIGIBLE.
contact.how_to_apply: FILL OUT AND SUBMIT THE ONLINE APPLICATION FORM AT WWW.PETALUMAROTARY.ORG. RESPOND WITH ALL REQUESTED INFORMATION, INCLUDING AN ITEMIZED BUDGET, AND ATTACH SUPPORTING INFORMATION AS NECESSARY. IN THE PROJECT SUMMARY AREA BE SURE TO ANSWER THE FOLLING QUESTIONS: A) WHAT WILL STUDENTS/CITIZENS EXPERIENCE AND WHAT DO YOU HOPE WILL BE THE OUTCOME AS A RESULT OF THE EXPERIENCE? B) HOW WILL THIS PROJECT IMPACT STUDENT SUCCESS OR SERVE A CURRENT NEED IN THE COMMUNITY? C) WHAT SEGMENT OF THE PETALLUMA AREA POPULATION WILL THIS GRANT SERVE (E.G. GRADE LEVEL/POPULATION, TOTAL NUMBER OF STUDENTS/INDIVIDUALD SERVED)? D)IF YOU EVER APPLIED FOR OR RECEIVED A ROTARY FOUNDATION GRANT IN THE PAST, PLEASE LIST THE GRANT AND YEAR OF APPLICATION/AWARD. E)IF THE TOTAL GRANT CANNOT BE FULLY FUNDED, WHAT IS THE MINIMUM AMOUNT OF ANY SUBMISSION DEADLINES.

comparable_grant.purpose: TO INCREASE CHARITABLE GIVING IN THE UNITED STATES.

contact.restrictions: Priority is given to smaller public charities located in the San Francisco Bay Area, including Sacramento, California. Education and human services are favored. The foregoing is not intended to be a complete list of priorities. Written solicitations only. The Foundation has no paid staff and does not acknowledge the receipt of applications. Successful applicants will be notified by mail, which mail will include a check for the amount of the grant authorized by the Foundation. Grants generally do not exceed $5,000.
contact.how_to_apply: A simple statement of the purpose of the proposed grant-how it will be used, what project will be supported, etc. Required application materials: 1. IRS tax exemption determination letter. The charity must be recognized as a public charity-not a private foundation. 2. The charity's employer identification number. 3. The charity's most recent financial statements showing: a. A statement of the charity's revenue and expense, showing the charity's sources of support and expenditures for its most recent year. Expense categories should include compensation of management and interest paid on loans secured by real property, if any. b. The charity's balance sheet. 4. The charity's budget for revenue and expense for the current year and, if available, the charity's budget for revenue and expense for the coming year. 5. The average percentage of donations to the charity used for fund raising during the last 2 years.
